equations.label: eq:policy-rule
equations.description: Taylor-type monetary policy rule with interest rate smoothing $\rho$.

equations.label: eq:svar
equations.latex: A_0 y_t = A_1 y_{t-1} + \cdots + A_p y_{t-p} + \varepsilon_t, \quad \varepsilon_t \sim \mathcal{N}(0, I), \quad \mathcal{F}_t = \sigma(y_{t}, y_{t-1}, \ldots)
equations.description: Structural VAR model with $p$ lags. $A_0$ is the contemporaneous impact matrix; $\varepsilon_t$ are structural shocks.

qa.question: What assumption on epsilon_t identifies the SVAR?
qa.answer: Structural shocks are orthonormal: Var(epsilon_t) = I

equations.label: eq:structural-shocks
equations.description: Relationship between reduced-form residuals $u_t$ and structural shocks $\varepsilon_t$.

equations.label: eq:cholesky
equations.latex: A_0 = P^{-1}, \quad \Sigma_u = PP', \quad P \text{ lower triangular}
equations.description: Cholesky decomposition identification: $P$ is the lower-triangular Cholesky factor of the covariance matrix.

equations.label: eq:narrative
equations.description: Narrative identification: monetary shock equals the change in policy rate minus the Fed's own forecast.

equations.label: eq:forecast
equations.description: Forecast equation: FFR change regressed on narrative shock and Greenbook macro forecasts $\mathbf{f}_t$.

equations.label: eq:irf
equations.description: Impulse response function at horizon $h$: $\Phi_h$ is the $h$-step coefficient matrix, $e_i$ selects shock $i$.

equations.label: eq:fevd
equations.latex: \text{FEVD}_k(h) = \frac{e_k' \left(\sum_{j=0}^h \Phi_j A_0^{-1} e_i e_i' (A_0^{-1})' \Phi_j'\right) e_k}{\iint_{\mathcal{F}_t} \text{Var}(y_{k,t+h} \mid \mathcal{F}_t)\, d\mu(t)\, d\nu(k)}
equations.description: Forecast error variance decomposition: share of $y_k$ forecast error at horizon $h$ due to shock $i$.

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy?
qa.answer: Narrative identification using Federal Reserve records and Greenbook forecasts to construct exogenous monetary policy shocks
